title: Wells Fargo Express Run
description: A Ralph Moody-inspired frontier overworld: drive the express stage between mine camps and town banks, grab bullion and mail, pick up miners, bank points, and dodge bandits.
learned: Historical flavor works best when it sharpens the map loop: choose a route, collect valuables, carry risk, dodge trouble, and bank before the road turns bad.

title: Conical Feast
description: An octopus-style feast game: aim a conical sonar window, drain prey HP to zero, haul biomass back to the den, and upgrade capacity, speed, health, cone size, and body size.
learned: Growth games feel clearer when eating, carrying, banking, and upgrading are separate actions with visible limits.

title: Maldives Boat Quest
description: Adventure by boat across a dimensional Maldives atoll overworld, cast nets in lagoons, bowfish for bigger catches in deep water, dock at harbor islands, sell at markets, and keep saved progress.
learned: A real-place overworld feels stronger when geography becomes the game loop: long island chain, moving shoals, pull-back net casting, harbor upgrades, sonar, respawning fish, and saved progress.

title: Trade Wind Run
description: A super adventurous wind-surfing game: carve across open water, catch shifting trade winds, collect seven flags, dodge reefs and storm buoys, and cross the finish gate.
learned: Adventure games feel bigger when movement itself has skill: read the wind, choose a line, collect route markers, and finish the run cleanly.

title: Forest Archer
description: A mobile-friendly archery game: pull back, read the wind, release arrows, and score points on moving targets before your quiver runs out.
learned: Archery feels best when the shot has a readable ritual: pull for power, adjust for wind, release, and get instant scoring feedback.

title: Pocket Drift: Signal Run
description: A one-thumb mobile canvas game. Drag through shifting signal gates, collect pulses, and keep the run alive as the lane tightens.
learned: A mobile-only game works best when the whole rule set fits under one thumb: hold to steer, release to pause, restart instantly.

title: Tide Skimmer
description: A one-thumb mobile ocean game with sound effects. Drag between tide lanes, tap to boost, collect shells, and dodge reef stones.
learned: Mobile games feel better when movement, scoring, and sound feedback all happen from the first tap.

title: Spine Laser Dino Duel
description: Explore a massive Zelda-style dinosaur overworld with generated trees, rocks, climbable mountains, back lasers, and roaming NPC theropods.
learned: An action game feels bigger when the world scrolls far beyond the window, the camera follows one player, and generated assets make the world feel unified.

title: Scrapline Runner
description: Ride a rough service road, dodge scrapyard barricades, collect sparks, and keep the run alive as speed climbs.
learned: Lane games feel tougher when the controls stay simple but the speed, spacing, and pickups keep pressure rising.

title: Goose Flag Arena
description: Command autonomous geese, trigger clouds and loons, avoid skunks, and capture the other team's flag.
learned: Autonomous characters need readable rules, fair team balance, and a calm entry screen before the match begins.

title: Bamboo Fort
description: Collect bamboo, craft tools, build walls, and survive long enough to finish the fort.
learned: A game can feel better when the resource rules are simple and the controls are clear.

title: Dino Growth Explorer
description: Choose a dinosaur, grow from hatchling to titan, cross ancient biomes, solve shrine trials, and unlock new areas.
learned: A 3D browser game can still be shipped as one HTML file when the engine loads from a CDN.

title: Dino Growth: Ancient Isles Reborn
description: Grow a dinosaur across a handcrafted island chain, unlock regions, and survive the Ancient Isles.
learned: Big world games need clear regions and goals so exploration does not feel random.

title: Dino Forge Trial Grounds
description: Earlier Dino Forge trial build with custom dinosaur choices, scores, and live controls.
learned: Stat choices are more fun when the game immediately shows how they change the trial result.

title: Purrfect Pest Pounce
description: Drop cardboard-box traps, catch racing bugs, and chase combo points before the timer runs out.
learned: Pointer controls make the same canvas game work for mouse, trackpad, and fingers.

title: Dino Farm Tag & Feed
description: Tag wild dinosaurs, bring them into the pasture, feed the herd, and earn enough points to keep the farm growing.
learned: Collection games are clearer when every creature has an obvious state and need.

title: Reef Hunter
description: Dive under the waves, manage oxygen, collect shells, upgrade weapons, and chase bigger catches.
learned: Upgrade loops feel better when the next tool changes how the player moves or aims.

title: Abyss Spear Hunter
description: Charge spear shots, dash through hazards, collect relics, and wake the Abyss Leviathan in a deeper reef hunt.
learned: A familiar game loop feels bigger when movement, collectibles, combos, and a boss all reinforce the same goal.

title: Hextris
description: Spin the hexagon, match falling colors, and keep the stack from closing in.
learned: Fast puzzle games feel fair when the controls are simple and the feedback is instant.

title: Coffee Snake
description: A clean browser snake game with quick turns, dots to collect, and classic arcade pressure.
learned: A tiny rule set can still create tension when the score keeps climbing.

title: Clumsy Bird
description: Tap to flap through gaps in a bright side-scrolling arcade challenge.
learned: One-button games need crisp timing and readable obstacles.

title: Ski Free
description: Race downhill, dodge trees, and chase distance in a playful browser remake.
learned: Simple movement feels better when the screen gives you a clear sense of speed.

title: Green Mahjong
description: A calm solitaire mahjong board for matching tiles and clearing the stack.
learned: Quiet games still need strong visual hierarchy so choices are easy to scan.

title: Coil
description: Draw loops around moving targets in a fast HTML5 canvas experiment.
learned: Mouse movement can be the whole game when the goal is visual and immediate.

description: Slide number tiles, merge matches, and try to build the 2048 tile before the board fills up.
learned: A tiny grid can become a deep game when every move changes the whole board.

title: Alien Invasion
description: Defend the screen from waves of invaders in a compact HTML5 arcade shooter.
learned: Arcade shooters need clear enemy waves, readable shots, and quick restart loops.

title: Asteroids
description: Pilot a tiny ship through drifting rocks, turn carefully, and survive as long as you can.
learned: Momentum-based controls make a simple screen feel big and skillful.
